content:
===BEGIN===
I showed up for what I thought was a straightforward interview at a tech startup. The receptionist handed me a VR headset and said, “Step into the future of hiring.” Skeptical, I put it on—and suddenly, I was trapped in a bizarre simulation. Instead of questions about my skills, I was dodging virtual fireballs, solving puzzles that made no sense, and being quizzed on company trivia nobody could possibly know. Halfway through, the headset glitched, looping the same weird message: “Innovation requires chaos.” When I finally ripped it off, the “interviewer” just shrugged and said, “That’s our culture.” I left knowing two things: one, if this is the future of hiring, I’m not ready for it; and two, sometimes the hype is just a mask for disorganization. So, if you think your last interview was bad—trust me, it could always be worse.  
===END===
